name: Cirq (Google Quantum Computing Framework)

auth.notes: Local simulation: no auth; Google Quantum Computing Service: GCP credentials

performance.notes: CPU simulation O(2^n) in qubits; GPU via qsim; real hardware via Google QCS

agent_readiness.notes: Google's quantum computing framework. import cirq. q0, q1 = cirq.LineQubit.range(2). circuit = cirq.Circuit(cirq.H(q0), cirq.CNOT(q0, q1), cirq.measure(q0, q1, key='result')). simulator = cirq.Simulator(). result = simulator.run(circuit, repetitions=100). result.histogram(key='result'). Noise models: cirq.depolarize. Parametrized circuits: cirq.Symbol. Transforms: cirq.optimize_for_target_gateset. cirq.protocols for interoperability. Google Sycamore processor. qsim integration for GPU simulation.

gotchas: Circuit model differs from Qiskit — not directly interchangeable
Real Google Quantum hardware requires GCP access and approval
qsim for GPU simulation requires separate install
Qubit addressing (GridQubit vs LineQubit) requires planning
best_when: Quantum algorithm prototyping with Google's Sycamore architecture in mind
Research on variational quantum algorithms (VQE, QAOA)
When Google Quantum Computing Service hardware access is available
avoid_when: When QASM compatibility with other frameworks is needed
summary: Cirq is Google's quantum computing framework for NISQ circuit design and simulation. cirq.Circuit(H(q0), CNOT(q0,q1), measure()). Simulator().run(circuit, repetitions=100). Noise models. Parametrized circuits. qsim for GPU simulation. Apache-2.0 by Google Quantum AI. For Google Sycamore architecture and GCP quantum hardware. Not directly compatible with Qiskit circuits. Real hardware requires GCP approval.
